metadata.official_name: University at Buffalo, The State University of New York

strategic_profile.executive_summary: The University at Buffalo (UB) is a premier, research-intensive public university and a flagship institution of the SUNY system. Known for its strong engineering, computer science, and health professions programs, UB offers excellent ROI with accessible tuition and high median graduate earnings. The university boasts three distinct campuses and vibrant Division I athletics, serving over 31,000 students in an inclusive environment.

analyst_takeaways.insight: UB's acceptance rate has climbed from 56% in 2018 to roughly 74% in 2024, showing an accommodation of growing applicant pools while maintaining its flagship public status.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants with solid academic records (a 3.7+ average) have a highly favorable chance of standard admission, though specific competitive majors like Computer Science require completing rigorous filter courses to fully declare.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Waitlist movement at UB is remarkably volatile. For the Class of 2026 (2022 admissions), the waitlist admit rate was a mere 1.4%, while for the Class of 2028 (2024 admissions), it hovered at 16.5%, and hit 33.9% in 2021.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants placed on the waitlist should remain cautiously optimistic but must have backup plans, as administrative decisions heavily depend on fluctuating yearly yield.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Median 10-year earnings for UB graduates sit at $71,430 against a median student debt of $19,000.
analyst_takeaways.implication: UB presents an outstanding return on investment, particularly for in-state students paying public tuition rates, rivaling the financial outcomes of much more expensive private universities.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: UB offers a vibrant campus life with over 400 student clubs and Division I athletics. There is a strong sense of school spirit ('True Blue'). The social scene is robust but heavily influenced by the winter weather, which pushes many activities indoors.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: The university is divided into three campuses: North (suburban Amherst, housing most undergrad programs), South (Northeast Buffalo, housing architecture and health sciences), and Downtown (medical). Transportation between them via shuttle is a significant part of student life.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: As an R1 institution and AAU member with over $400M in annual research expenditures, undergraduates have ample opportunities to engage in high-level research across STEM, medical, and social science fields.

current_status.test_policy_details: UB does not require SAT and ACT test scores for admissions and scholarship consideration (SUNY-wide test-optional policy). Students can choose to share scores for holistic review.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist admit rate is steady from 2023 but drastically different from older cycles.
Extremely low waitlist clearance due to high regular yield.
COVID-19 pandemic effects led to unusually high waitlist usage.

gpa.notes: Average high school GPA is around 3.70.

testing.policy_note: Test-optional since Fall 2020 following SUNY guidelines.

colleges.name: School of Engineering and Applied Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking rigorous technical training with abundant research opportunities. A highly regarded school within SUNY.
colleges.housing_profile: Standard campus dormitories; engineering living-learning communities available.
colleges.student_archetype: Technically minded, focused, and willing to tackle heavy workloads and rigorous weeder courses.

majors.name: Computer Science
weeder_courses: CSE 115 Introduction to Computer Science I
CSE 116 Introduction to Computer Science II
majors.special_requirements: Combined BS/MS requires a 3.3 GPA among all required CSE, MTH, and EAS courses to apply.

core_courses: CSE 115 Introduction to Computer Science I
CSE 116 Introduction to Computer Science II

majors.notes: Students are admitted as 'intended' majors and must pass the prerequisite sequence with a minimum 2.8 UB GPA to formally declare.

colleges.name: School of Management
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Great for corporate-focused students aiming for careers in finance, accounting, or management in the Northeast.

majors.name: Business Administration, Management and Operations
majors.notes: Most popular undergraduate major at the university.

colleges.name: College of Arts and Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students pursuing humanities, natural sciences, social sciences, or those exploring undeclared pathways.
colleges.housing_profile: Most undergraduate activity is centered on the North Campus where residence halls are located.
colleges.student_archetype: Academically curious students seeking a traditional liberal arts education paired with R1 university resources.

majors.name: Psychology
majors.notes: Second most popular major at the university by volume of graduates.

application_deadlines.notes: Nonbinding Early Action. Candidates receive early response but have until May 1 to deposit.
Rolling admissions process until capacity is reached.

supplemental_requirements.details: A personal essay outlining academic goals is generally optional but highly recommended as part of the holistic review.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: UB explicitly notes evaluating documented creative talent, demonstrated leadership, community service, and overcoming special life circumstances.

major_selection_tactics: For highly rigorous programs like Computer Science or Engineering, entering as 'Expressed Interest/Intended Major' is common. Ensure high grades in early calculus and intro courses.
Apply Early Action (Nov 1) to maximize access to merit scholarships and honors programs.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Students applying to restricted/impacted majors within Engineering who do not initially qualify may be admitted to the university as an intended major and must pass gateway courses to formally declare.

scholarships.name: Presidential Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Most selective and highly valued award in the SUNY system. Covers tuition, fees, and includes Honors College admission.

scholarships.name: Provost Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Renewable four-year award applied to direct costs of attendance.

scholarships.name: Pride of New York Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Available to high-achieving first-year domestic students from NY State to cover non-tuition costs like fees.

scholarships.name: International Student Flagship Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Renewable award for incoming international first-year students.

scholarships.name: Alison L. Des Forges Memorial Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Targeted for Buffalo Public or Charter School graduates demonstrating commitment to human rights.

ap_policy.general_rule: An official score report showing a minimum score of 3 will guarantee elective credit.
exceptions: AP Calculus has a maximum award of 8 credits total.
Some specific major or general education requirements may demand a 4 or 5.
ap_policy.usage: Elective credit, major requirements, or UB Curriculum (General Education) requirements.

ib_policy.general_rule: Scores of 5 or higher on Higher Level (HL) exams guarantee credit.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Students must complete at least one semester at UB before applying to combined BS/MS programs.

what_it_takes: Solid academic foundation (mostly A's/B's with a ~3.7 average).
Demonstrated commitment to extracurriculars or overcoming personal challenges (holistic review).
essay_tips: Not strictly required, but provides vital context for holistic admissions.
red_flags: Inability to handle large lecture environments (especially in intro STEM classes).
Falling behind in rigorous early sequence courses (Calc 1/2, Intro CS) for intended engineering majors.
insights: Students highly praise the ROI and research opportunities but caution about navigating administrative bureaucracy and harsh Buffalo winters.
The campus is split; the North Campus feels like its own suburban city, while the South Campus is closer to the city and older.
